heading: Command: commissioned officers in certain designated categories
label: 9229 Command: commissioned officers in certain designated categories
text: An officer designated as a medical, dental, veterinary, medical service, or biomedical sciences officer or as a nurse is not entitled to exercise command because of rank, except within the categories prescribed in subsection (a), (b), (c), (d), (e), (f), or (i) of , or over persons placed under his charge.section 9067 of this title
notes: Historical and Revision Notes1956ActRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)8579(a)10:82.R.S. 1169.8579(b)10:166e (less 1st sentence).(less 1st sentence), .Apr. 16, 1947, ch. 38, § 10661 Stat. 44In subsection (a), the words “Except as provided in ”, not contained in section 1169 of the Revised Statutes, but contained in the United States Code, are omitted as surplusage, since 10:94 deals exclusively with assignments. The words “except within the categories prescribed in section 8067(a)–(d) of this title” are substituted for the words “in the line or in other staff corps”. Air Force nurses and women medical specialists are not covered by subsection (a), since their command authority is specifically stated in subsection (b).section 94 of this titleIn subsection (b), the words “may exercise command only” are substituted for the words “shall not be entitled * * * to command except”. The words “by virtue of their rank” and “by competent authority” are omitted as surplusage. 10:166(e) (last 22 words of last sentence) is omitted as superseded by .section 8012(e) of this title

1958ActRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)8579(b)10 App.:166b–3., .Aug. 9, 1955, ch. 654, § 169 Stat. 579This amendment reflects the authority contained in section 8067(e) and (f) of this title to appoint male reserve officers with a view to designation as Air Force nurses or medical specialists.

Amendments2018— renumbered as this section and substituted “section 9067” for “section 8067”.Pub. L. 115–232section 8579 of this title1980— substituted provision prohibiting an officer designated as a medical, dental, veterinary, medical service, or biomedical sciences officer or as a nurse from exercising command because of rank, except within the categories prescribed in section 8067(a) to (f) or (i) of this title, or over persons placed under his charge for provision prohibiting an officer designated as a medical, dental, veterinary, or medical service officer from exercising command because of rank, except within categories prescribed in section 8067(a) to (d) of this title, and authorizing an Air Force nurse or medical specialist to exercise command only within his category, or over persons placed under his charge.Pub. L. 96–5131958—Subsec. (b). struck out “woman” before “medical specialist”, and substituted “his” for “her” in two places.Pub. L. 85–861

Effective Date of 2018 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with provision for the coordination of amendments and special rule for certain redesignations, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 115–232section 800 of Pub. L. 115–232section 3001 of this titleFeb. 1, 2019
